scenario.context: Station 06 of the day run: test user interfaces and evaluate UX.
scenario.mission: You choose test methods, formulate UX test cases, and secure improvements through follow-up tests.

constraints: Keep test cases tied to requirements

customerRequest.subject: Station 06: UX test and follow-up test
customerRequest.body: Please run structured UX tests and document the findings together with the follow-up test.

questions.title: Define the test goal
questions.prompt: What should a UX testing process start with?

options.text: With a final report before the test run
With clear test goals and core scenarios

feedback.correct: Correct: Clear goals are the starting point.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without test goals, the evaluation stays vague.

questions.title: Finding documentation in the UX test
questions.prompt: Which details does a UX finding need? (Multiple choice)

options.text: The scenario and step where the defect occurs
The impact on the user's task
Only a subjective feeling without context

feedback.correct: Correct: The finding documentation is set up in a controllable way.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure context, impact, and prioritization more strongly.

questions.title: UX improvement cycle
questions.prompt: Put the flow from test to effectiveness check in order.

feedback.correct: Correct: The cycle is built cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please place finding capture and prioritization before correction and follow-up testing.

questions.title: Follow-up test rate
questions.prompt: Out of 19 prioritized UX findings, 15 were confirmed as fixed in the follow-up test. What is the rate in percent (rounded)?

feedback.wrong: Please calculate the confirmed share correctly.

questions.title: Summarize the UX evaluation
questions.prompt: Formulate a UX evaluation in 4-6 sentences with a strength, the main problem, and the next step.
questions.expectedOutline: Balanced evaluation with a clear follow-up action.

feedback.correct: Correct: The evaluation is directly usable for the team.
feedback.wrong: Please structure the strength, the main problem, and the next step more clearly.
